name: Publish to crates.io with Proper Metadata
description: Prepare, validate, and publish a Rust crate to crates.io with complete metadata, documentation, and versioning.
prerequisites: A Cargo project ready for publishing

steps.title: Login to crates.io
steps.description: Authenticate with crates.io using your API token from the website.
steps.tip: Get your token from https://crates.io/settings/tokens. The token is stored in ~/.cargo/credentials.toml.

steps.title: Ensure required metadata is set
steps.description: Verify that Cargo.toml has all fields required for publishing.
steps.command: cargo read-manifest | jq '{name, version, description, license, repository}'
steps.warning: Publishing requires at minimum: name, version, description, and license. Missing fields will cause publish to fail.

steps.title: Generate and review documentation
steps.description: Build documentation locally and verify it looks correct before publishing.

steps.title: Dry-run the publish
steps.description: Validate the crate package without actually uploading it.
steps.tip: This catches missing files, invalid metadata, and build errors before the real publish.

steps.title: Publish the crate
steps.description: Upload the crate to crates.io for the world to use.
steps.warning: Published versions cannot be deleted or overwritten. Double-check the version number before publishing.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Publish Rust Crate to crates.io
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Pre-Publish Checklist ==="
echo "Checking metadata..."
cargo read-manifest | jq '{name, version, description, license, repository}' 2>/dev/null || cargo metadata --format-version 1 | jq '.packages[0] | {name, version, description, license, repository}'

echo ""
echo "=== Running Tests ==="
cargo test

echo ""
echo "=== Clippy Check ==="
cargo clippy -- -D warnings

echo ""
echo "=== Dry Run ==="
cargo publish --dry-run

echo ""
echo "=== Ready to Publish ==="
echo "Run 'cargo publish' to upload to crates.io"
echo ""
echo "=== Publish Commands ==="
echo "Login:    cargo login"
echo "Dry run:  cargo publish --dry-run"
echo "Publish:  cargo publish"
echo "Yank:     cargo yank --version 1.0.0"
echo "Unyank:   cargo yank --version 1.0.0 --undo"

faq.question: Can I unpublish a crate version?
faq.answer: You cannot delete a published version. You can 'yank' it with 'cargo yank --version 1.0.0', which prevents new projects from depending on it but does not break existing users.

faq.question: How do I include extra files in the published crate?
faq.answer: By default, Cargo includes all tracked Git files. Use the 'include' or 'exclude' fields in Cargo.toml to customize which files are packaged. Run 'cargo package --list' to see what will be included.

faq.question: How does crates.io documentation work?
faq.answer: docs.rs automatically builds and hosts documentation for every published crate. It uses the latest stable Rust compiler. Add a [package.metadata.docs.rs] section in Cargo.toml to customize the build.
